description: Field definitions shared by the personal hypertrophy, progression, nutrition, and recovery analysis schemas.

conventions.stable_identifiers: Persistent lowercase snake_case identifiers matching ^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$. Identifiers must not depend on output row order. Source-derived record IDs should be deterministic across reruns.
conventions.research_crosswalks: research_exercise_id and research_muscle_group_id are nullable. Populate them only for a confident semantic match; preserve personal variation and equipment identity separately.
conventions.dates: Calendar dates use ISO YYYY-MM-DD. Timestamps use ISO 8601 with an explicit offset or Z. source_date_range endpoints are inclusive.
conventions.missing_values: Unknown values are null in JSON and empty in CSV. Never replace an unknown value with zero. missing_data_flags explains material gaps.
conventions.confidence: confidence_level is categorical and confidence_score is 0-100. The future integration envelope uses 0-1 confidence values because they act as weights rather than display scores.
conventions.ranges: Range objects use inclusive min and max fields. Broad or null endpoints are preferred over unsupported precision.
conventions.source_references: Every derived result identifies the raw or normalized source. A reference may identify a path, worksheet, row, source record, and source date without embedding source data.
conventions.future_weighting: Within an integration_envelope, personal_weight plus research_weight should equal 1 within floating-point tolerance. Missing research evidence uses an empty research_default, research_confidence 0, and research_weight 0.
conventions.units: Units are encoded in field names where practical or recorded in an adjacent unit field. Loads are not comparable across unconfirmed machines merely because the unit is the same.

analysis_id.description: Persistent identifier for one reproducible analysis run.

manifest_id.description: Persistent identifier for an output manifest.

prescription_id.description: Stable identifier for one exercise-and-muscle-specific prescription.

rule_id.description: Stable identifier for one recovery decision rule.

output_id.description: Stable identifier for one normalized or derived output artifact.

exercise_id.description: Personal canonical exercise identifier; variation and equipment instance remain separate analytical dimensions.

research_exercise_id.description: Crosswalk to research_database exercise_id when the match is confident; otherwise null.

research_muscle_group_id.description: Crosswalk to research_database muscle_group_id when regional correspondence is confident; otherwise null.

source_date_range.description: Inclusive start_date and end_date covered by the observations supporting the result. Either endpoint may be null when no dated source exists.

start_date.description: Earliest dated observation represented by the result.

end_date.description: Latest dated observation represented by the result.

analysis_date.description: Calendar date on which the result was calculated.

generated_at.description: Exact timestamp at which the analysis artifact was generated.

methodology_version.description: Version of normalization, linkage, scoring, and recommendation rules used.

pipeline_version.description: Version of executable pipeline code when tracked separately from methodology.

sample_size.description: Number of observations supporting the result. The observation unit must be clear from the table or evidence summary.

confidence_level.description: Categorical personal-evidence confidence after sample size, duration, completeness, variation consistency, and confounding are considered.

confidence_score.description: Numeric data-confidence score; null when the method does not calculate one.

missing_data_flags.description: Machine-readable flags for material missingness or unsupported fields; use an empty array when none apply.

source_references.description: Traceable references to raw or normalized inputs supporting the result.

notes.description: Assumptions, caveats, discrepancies, and interpretation notes; use an empty array when none apply.

source_id.description: Stable identifier for a source file or source dataset.

source_record_id.description: Native or deterministically derived source record identifier.

source_row.description: One-based source row number when meaningful.

source_date.description: Measurement, workout, meal, or biometric date represented by the referenced source record.

database_name.description: Human-readable name of the personal data product.

research_database_version.description: Version of the external research database available to the run; null when it was not used.

analysis_scope.description: Domains and date periods included in the run.

config_paths.description: Configuration files used to reproduce the run.

table_record_counts.description: Record count for each normalized and derived output.

data_quality_summary.description: Run-level summary of duplicate, missing, invalid, and inconsistent records.

personal_research_weighting_policy.description: Policy for increasing personal evidence weight as observation quality and quantity grow.

role.description: Program role assigned to the exercise for the specified muscle group.

recommended_sets_per_session.description: Recommended working-set range per exposure; warm-ups are excluded.

recommended_weekly_sets.description: Recommended normal weekly hard-set range for the exercise and muscle context.

recommended_rep_range.description: Meaningful personal repetition range rather than a single-repetition target.

progression_rule.description: Concrete rule for progressing load, repetitions, or execution.

observed_best_range.description: Conditions directly observed with the best combined progression and recovery.

recommended_future_range.description: Forward-looking prescription after personal evidence, uncertainty, and guardrails are applied.

highest_recoverable_range_observed.description: Highest observed volume or intensity range tolerated without repeated adverse outcomes; not automatically recommended.

evidence_summary.description: Concise explanation of personal evidence and material limitations behind the prescription.

warning_signs.description: Patterns indicating the exercise may be becoming unproductive or excessively costly.

scope.description: Whether the rule affects a full session, one exercise, or one muscle group.

action.description: Training adjustment recommended when the trigger logic is satisfied.

signals.description: Named recovery, performance, nutrition, and recent-volume indicators evaluated by the rule.

minimum_converging_indicators.description: Minimum independent signals required; prevents a major adjustment from one isolated biometric.

trigger_logic.description: Deterministic Boolean or JSON-Logic-style expression over signal IDs.

action_parameters.description: Load, set, volume, RPE, timing, or substitution parameters for the action.

historical_evidence.description: Eligible observations, triggered observations, outcomes, and effect summary supporting the rule.

table_name.description: Logical normalized or derived table name.

relative_path.description: Repository-relative path to the generated artifact.

schema_path.description: Repository-relative JSON Schema or table-contract path.

row_count.description: Physical row or item count in the output.

stable_identifier_columns.description: Columns that uniquely and reproducibly identify output records.

columns.description: Ordered output column names when applicable.

checksum_sha256.description: Optional lowercase SHA-256 checksum of the generated artifact.

integration_envelope.description: Forward-compatible personal/research recommendation blend. When present, all ten child fields are required.

personal_estimate.description: Recommendation or range estimated only from personal evidence.

personal_confidence.description: Quality-adjusted confidence in the personal estimate.

personal_sample_size.description: Comparable personal observations contributing to the estimate.

research_default.description: Applicable external research default; use an empty object when none exists.

research_confidence.description: Confidence assigned to the applicable research default.

personal_weight.description: Decision weight assigned to personal evidence.

research_weight.description: Decision weight assigned to research evidence.

final_recommendation.description: Recommendation after applying the evidence hierarchy and weights.

weighting_reason.description: Human-readable explanation of the personal/research weights.

last_updated.description: Date on which the integration envelope was last recalculated.
